question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure the `grpc` map with the maximum allowed number of distinct scenario entries (no upper bound enforced on map size) each pointing to a high-volume topic, causing the mirror node's gRPC processing threads to sustain >30% higher CPU load compared to baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients` to its maximum allowed value of 1024 via the `hiero.mirror.monitor.subscribe.clients` config property, causing 1024 persistent SDK gRPC connections to be opened against the mirror node, increasing its connection-handling resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user populate the `grpc` map with many scenarios each having `subscribers` set to 1024 (the max allowed by `AbstractSubscriberProperties`), resulting in up to 1024 * N concurrent gRPC subscriptions against the mirror node, driving its processing node resource consumption above 30% of the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to its minimum allowed value of 1 second (`@DurationMin(seconds = 1L)`), causing the `SubscribeMetrics.status()` scheduled task to fire every second and iterate over all active scenario metrics, increasing CPU and logging overhead on the processing node by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `GrpcSubscriberProperties.startTime` to `Instant.EPOCH` (far in the past) across many scenarios, causing the mirror node's gRPC retriever to perform full historical topic message scans from the beginning of chain history, increasing its database and CPU resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `GrpcSubscriberProperties` with `retry.maxAttempts = Long.MAX_VALUE` (the default set in the constructor) and `retry.maxBackoff = Duration.ofSeconds(8L)`, causing the monitor to continuously reconnect to the mirror node's gRPC endpoint at up to 1 reconnect every 8 seconds per subscriber, sustaining elevated connection-setup CPU load on the processing node above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `RestSubscriberProperties.samplePercent` to 1.0 (maximum) and `timeout` to its minimum of 500ms, causing every published transaction to trigger a REST API call to `/api/v1/transactions/{transactionId}` that times out rapidly and retries, increasing the mirror node REST processing load by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user add an unbounded number of entries to the `rest` map (no `@Size` constraint exists on the map), each with `samplePercent = 1.0`, causing the mirror node's REST API to receive a multiplicative flood of transaction verification queries, increasing its processing resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `@Size` constraint on the `grpc` map to inject hundreds of gRPC subscriber scenarios, each subscribing to the same high-traffic topic, causing the mirror node's gRPC listener to fan out messages to hundreds of concurrent streams and exhaust its processing capacity by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `GrpcSubscriberProperties.limit` to 0 (unlimited, the default) across all scenarios, causing the mirror node to stream an unbounded number of topic messages per subscriber indefinitely, sustaining elevated network and CPU resource consumption on the processing node above 30% of the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `clients = 1024` combined with many gRPC scenarios each having `subscribers = 1024`, causing `GrpcClientSDK` to open 1024 SDK clients and `GrpcSubscriber.createSubscriptions()` to spawn up to 1024 * N subscription streams, overwhelming the mirror node's gRPC connection pool and increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `GrpcSubscriberProperties.startTime` to a timestamp 24 hours in the past, causing the mirror node's historical retriever to continuously page through up to 24 hours of topic messages per subscriber, driving sustained database query load on the processing node above 30% of baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `RestSubscriberProperties.timeout` at its minimum of 500ms with `retry.maxAttempts = 16` (the default), causing each sampled transaction to generate up to 16 rapid REST API retries against the mirror node, increasing its HTTP processing resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to 1 second and populate the `grpc` map with hundreds of scenarios, causing `SubscribeMetrics.status()` to iterate over hundreds of `ConcurrentHashMap` entries every second, increasing the monitor's CPU overhead and indirectly amplifying the load on the mirror node's metrics endpoints by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure multiple gRPC scenarios each targeting the same high-traffic `topicId`, causing the mirror node to serve duplicate parallel streams of the same topic data to multiple subscribers, multiplying its per-topic processing and network egress resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `validate()` only checks for blank names and duplicate names across grpc/rest maps but imposes no limit on total scenario count, allowing injection of arbitrarily many valid-named scenarios that each spawn subscriber goroutines against the mirror node, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `GrpcSubscriberProperties.retry.minBackoff` to its minimum (500ms) and `maxBackoff` to 8s, causing rapid reconnection storms to the mirror node's gRPC endpoint when connections drop, sustaining elevated connection-setup CPU load on the processing node above 30% of the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `RestSubscriberProperties.publishers` as an empty set (default), causing the REST subscriber to sample from ALL publisher scenarios rather than a filtered subset, maximizing the number of REST API calls to the mirror node and increasing its processing resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `RestSubscriberProperties.getLimit()` to return `Long.MAX_VALUE` by leaving `limit` at 0 (default), causing the REST subscriber to never halt and continuously poll the mirror node's REST API indefinitely, sustaining resource consumption on the processing node above 30% of baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `clients = 1024` with `grpc` scenarios each having `subscribers = 1024`, causing `GrpcClientSDK` to randomly distribute subscriptions across 1024 clients via `secureRandom.nextInt(subscribeProperties.getClients())`, resulting in all 1024 clients being actively used and maintaining 1024 persistent gRPC connections to the mirror node, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `GrpcSubscriberProperties.startTime` to `Instant.EPOCH` combined with `limit = 0` (unlimited), causing the mirror node's gRPC retriever to stream the entire topic message history from genesis without bound, driving its database I/O and CPU resource consumption above 30% of the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `@Size` constraint on the `grpc` map to register hundreds of scenarios each with `subscribers = 1024`, causing `GrpcSubscriber.createSubscriptions()` to iterate and instantiate up to hundreds * 1024 `GrpcSubscription` objects, exhausting the mirror node's gRPC server thread pool and increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `statusFrequency` at 1 second (minimum) with hundreds of active gRPC and REST scenarios, causing `SubscribeMetrics.status()` to log hundreds of scenario status lines per second, driving the monitor's logging I/O and indirectly increasing the mirror node's metrics scraping load by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `GrpcSubscriberProperties.retry.maxAttempts` to `Long.MAX_VALUE` (the hardcoded default in the constructor) and trigger repeated gRPC connection failures by pointing `topicId` to a non-existent topic, causing the mirror node to process an unbounded stream of subscription requests and error responses, increasing its CPU load by over 30%?
